Skip to content

Matt Bierner

Hi, I'm Matt Bierner. I make tools for developers, software for creating, and art for fun.

Blog Contact

Projects

iOS

iOS apps and Augmented Reality projects

Phonogram

2023

—  Share audio messages with AR effects.

links App Store Documentation

Reality Filters

2021

—  Paint image filters onto the world in AR.

links App Store Support

VoxelizeAR

2021

—  Voxelize the world in AR.

links App Store Support

Face Drop

2021

—  Make augmented reality selfies.

link

Headshot

2021

—  Capture realtime face textures with ARKit.

links Source Notes

warpAR

2021

—  Warp the visible world in AR.

links App Store Documentation

Pic Plane

2021

—  Create AR photo collages and perspective effects.

links App Store Documentation

watAR

2021

—  Ripple real-world surfaces like water.

links App Store Documentation

Beatsy

2020

—  Visualize music in AR.

links App Store Documentation

In The Walls

2020

—  Put your face into walls with AR.

links App Store Reality shaders example

swapiies

2018

—  Swap live camera views with a nearby friend.

link

VS Code

Projects from nearly a decade on the VS Code team.

Copilot Codebase Knowledge

2026 - Present

—  Ground Copilot answers in workspace context.

link

Markdown Language Service

2022 - Present

—  Power rich Markdown editing across tools.

link

Code 20/20

2021

—  Share short VS Code video tips.

link

VS Code Docs View

2020

—  Show documentation in the VS Code sidebar.

links Extension Source

Gif Player

2020

—  Play, pause, and scrub gifs in VS Code.

links Extension Source

GitHub Markdown Preview

2018

—  Bring GitHub-style Markdown rendering to VS Code.

links Extension Source Styling

Strict Null Checked the VS Code Codebase

2019

—  Incrementally enable strict null checks across VS Code.

link

VS Code Webview APIs

2018 - Present

—  Design and build VS Code's webview APIs.

links Webview API Webview Views Custom Editors

API Design

2018 - Present

—  Design VS Code extension APIs.

links API Reference Proposed APIs

Markdown Preview Mermaid Support

2017

—  Render Mermaid diagrams in VS Code Markdown previews.

link

VS Code TypeScript Language Features

2016 - Present

—  Develop VS Code's JavaScript and TypeScript language features.

link

VS Code Markdown Extension

2016 - Present

—  Build VS Code's Markdown previews, language features, and notebook support.

links Source Docs

Experiments

Small tools, toys, and experiments

Page Stack

2026

—  Compose pages from fragments of classic books.

links Open app How to use it

Gif Shift

2020

—  Shift gif pixels through time.

links Open app Source

Gif Mask

2020

—  Remix gifs with image masks.

links Open app Source

Gif Cube

2018

—  Turn animated gifs into physical 3D cubes.

links Website Source

Recurss

2018

—  Build recursive CSS specimens from nested divs.

links Website Submit specimen

Gif Slice

2018

—  Turn animated gifs into sliced 2D prints.

links Website Source

char flip

2018

—  Flip a single character in a tweet.

links Open app Source

reflow

2017

—  Resize images by reflowing pixels.

link

interleave.gif

2017

—  Interleave two gifs frame by frame.

link

Post Mortem

2017

—  Visualize Wikipedia edits after someone dies.

link

cube.gif

2016

—  View gifs as 3D volumes.

links Open app Source

median.gif

2016

—  Median-blend animated gifs.

links Open app Source

scanline.gif

2016

—  Flatten animated gifs into slit-scan images.

links Open app Source

Forward Propagation

2016

—  Explore history through human generations.

links Website Source

Vernacular

2016

—  Find Urban Dictionary entries inside longer works.

links Website Source

Symphony of Death

2016

—  Generate musical instruments from Halo 5 match data.

links Website Source

Art

Non-Flesh Tokens

2022

—  Assemble image NFTs from portrait blocks.

links OpenSea Visual browser

Lekker

2018

—  Control a vibrator using face tracking.

link

The Theremin-X Project

2018

—  Translate theremin input into wearable haptic feedback.

links Video Source

Nuke Scan

2018

—  Strip-scan nuclear test films.

link

Hue Theremin

2018

—  Control Hue lights with theremin MIDI input.

link

blueframe

2017

—  Interrupt gifs with a single blue frame.

links Open app Source

Tech

Libraries and utilities and other technical projects.

Akh

2016

—  Compose JavaScript monads and monad transformers.

links Source Documentation

Hamt+

2016

—  Add transient mutation to hash array mapped tries.

links Source Documentation

Hamt

2016

—  Implement immutable hash array mapped tries in JavaScript.

links npm Source

Apep

2015

—  Generate text with Dada Engine-style grammars.

links Source Documentation

Khepri

2014

—  Explore expression-oriented ECMAScript syntax.

link

Atum

2014

—  Interpret ECMAScript semantics in JavaScript.

link

Neith

2014

—  Navigate lazy JavaScript structures with zippers.

link

Parse.js

2013

—  Build incremental monadic parser combinators in JavaScript.

link

Letterpress

Burn For Him

2020

—  Print full-color Christmas cards by letterpress.

HE LIVED

2019

—  Laser-cut acrylic for a letterpress tribute print.

You Ain't No Punk You Punk

2019

—  Tape-letter a black-paper letterpress poster.

Bring Out the Gump

2019

—  Print a forest-themed letterpress exchange card.

Come play with me inside the fire

2019

—  Print a six-pass apple with metal type.

XFL 2020

2019

—  Print a future-football poster from linoleum blocks.

Rhonda's Workout

2019

—  Print an Aerobicide poster on hot pink paper.

Aerobicide Poster

2019

—  Print a letterpress poster for Aerobicide.

DNA Print

2019

—  Print the human genome onto punched paper tape.

links Writing Open house Conclusion

American Heat

2019

—  Print a four-pass movie-inspired card.

Two Eye Characters

2018

—  Print letterpress eye-character cards.

Fun

mandō

2026

—  Recast the top 100 Unix commands as vibe-coded SaaS companies.

link

FU: Future You

2019

—  Generate posts that sabotage your future self.

links Open app Source

GIFAKIRA

2018

—  Browse every Akira shot as gifs.

links Website Stream

The John Barleycorn Challenge

2017

—  Turn Jack London's John Barleycorn into a drinking game.

links Open app Source

Zork.git

2016

—  Play Zork through GitHub pull requests and commits.

links Game repo Code

allthecolors.gif

2016

—  Pack every 24-bit RGB color into gifs.

links Rainbow Array Rainbow Stack Rainbow Walk

Na Naa, Na Na Na Na Naa Naa Naa Na

2016

—  Visualize Katamari controller inputs on expanding spheres.

links Website Documentation Source

{{dubious}}

2016

—  Surface questionable Wikipedia cleanup-tag sentences.

links Source Dubious Results

80x40

2015

—  Play a collaborative ASCII canvas through GitHub pull requests.

links Canvas Repo Client Code
